projects.title: Unit 10 Project — Volume & Surface Area in Action
projects.desc: Apply volume and surface area to a real-world capstone challenge for Unit 10.

projects.title: Surface Area Review
projects.desc: Practice finding surface area from nets and 3D figures (6.G.4).

projects.title: Tangram Architect
projects.desc: Compose and decompose shapes to reason about area of faces (6.G.4).

projects.title: Unit 10 Graphic Novel — Part 2
projects.desc: Interactive graphic novel continuing the surface area story.

title: Surface Area of Pyramids
contentObjective: I can find the surface area of a pyramid by adding the base area and the lateral faces.
languageObjective: I can explain my work using the words pyramid, slant height, lateral face, and base.

turnAndTalk.question: A square pyramid time capsule display has a square base and four triangular faces that meet at a point on top. To find its surface area, which parts do you need to measure?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Surface area of a pyramid is the base area plus the areas of all the triangular lateral faces.

stems.en: I need the area of the ___ plus the areas of the ___ triangular faces.
stems.es: Necesito el área de la ___ más las áreas de las ___ caras triangulares.

stems.en: The surface area is the ___ area plus the ___ faces.
stems.es: El área de superficie es el área de la ___ más las caras ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students explain a square pyramid's surface area = base area + the four triangular lateral faces, and that you add every face together.
turnAndTalk.extend: Why do you use the slant height, not the pyramid's vertical height, to find the area of each triangular face?
extendStems: I use the slant height because it is the ___ of each triangular face.
The vertical height would not work because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: For Display A (square base 6×6, slant height 8), how did you combine the base area and the four triangular faces to get the total surface area?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Add the base area to four times the area of one triangular face (½ × base × slant height).

stems.en: The base area is ___ × ___ = ___, and one triangular face is ½ × 6 × 8 = ___.
stems.es: El área de la base es ___ × ___ = ___, y una cara triangular es ½ × 6 × 8 = ___.

stems.en: The total surface area is the base plus 4 × ___ = ___ in².
stems.es: El área total de superficie es la base más 4 × ___ = ___ in².

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute base = 36 in², one face = 24 in², four faces = 96 in², total SA = 132 in², distinguishing base from lateral faces.
turnAndTalk.extend: Two pyramids have the same 8×8 base but different slant heights. How will their surface areas compare, and which part stays the same?
extendStems: The pyramid with the larger slant height has ___ surface area because ___.
The ___ area stays the same because slant height only affects ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A museum builds a square pyramid display case with base edge 3 ft and slant height 4 ft, and the base is open (no glass). Talk through how to find how much glass the 4 triangular sides need.
turnAndTalk.kernel: Find one triangular face area, multiply by 4, and skip the base since it is open.

stems.en: Each triangular face has area ½ × ___ × ___ = ___ ft².
stems.es: Cada cara triangular tiene área ½ × ___ × ___ = ___ ft².

stems.en: Four faces need ___ ft² of glass, and the base needs ___ because it is open.
stems.es: Cuatro caras necesitan ___ ft² de vidrio, y la base necesita ___ porque está abierta.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute one face = ½ × 3 × 4 = 6 ft², four faces = 24 ft², and explain the open base is not counted, so only the lateral area matters.
turnAndTalk.extend: How does leaving the base open change which formula you use, and how would the answer change if the base needed glass too?
extendStems: Leaving the base open means I use only the ___ area because ___.
If the base needed glass, I would add ___ to the total.

turnAndTalk.question: A classmate found a square pyramid's surface area by adding only the four triangular faces. What did they forget, and how would you correct it?
turnAndTalk.kernel: They left out the base area, which must be added to the lateral faces for total surface area.

stems.en: They forgot to add the ___ area to the triangular faces.
stems.es: Olvidaron sumar el área de la ___ a las caras triangulares.

stems.en: The correct total surface area is the base plus the ___ lateral faces.
stems.es: El área de superficie total correcta es la base más las ___ caras laterales.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students identify the missing base area and state that total surface area = base area + all lateral (triangular) faces.
turnAndTalk.extend: When is it correct to leave out the base (like an open display case), and when must it be included? Generalize a rule.
extendStems: You leave out the base when ___, but include it when ___.
In general, total surface area of a pyramid is always ___.

vocabulary.definition: A solid with a flat bottom and triangle sides that meet at one point on top.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un sólido con un fondo plano y lados triangulares que se unen en un punto arriba.
vocabulary.visual: Like the Great Pyramid of Giza — a square on the bottom, 4 triangles slanting up to a point
vocabulary.cloze: A solid with a polygon base and triangular faces meeting at one point is a ___.

examples.text: A square base with four triangle sides meeting at a top point
examples.why: Its triangular faces meet at one apex.

examples.text: A box with six rectangular faces
examples.why: That is a rectangular prism, not a pyramid.

vocabulary.definition: The height of a side triangle, measured along its slanted face.
vocabulary.definitionEs: La altura de un triángulo lateral, medida por su cara inclinada.
vocabulary.visual: If you slide your finger from the bottom edge up the triangle face to the top point — that distance is the slant height
vocabulary.cloze: The height of a triangular face of a pyramid, measured along the slanted side, is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: A triangle side of a pyramid, not the bottom.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un lado triangular de una pirámide, no el fondo.
vocabulary.visual: A square pyramid has 4 lateral faces — one triangle for each side of the square base
vocabulary.cloze: A triangular side face of a pyramid is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: The flat bottom of a pyramid.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El fondo plano de una pirámide.
vocabulary.visual: A square pyramid sits on a square base; base area = side × side
vocabulary.cloze: The polygon on the bottom of a pyramid is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: The point at the top of a pyramid where the sides meet.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El punto en la parte de arriba de una pirámide donde se unen los lados.
vocabulary.visual: The pointy top of a pyramid — all the slanted edges connect here
vocabulary.cloze: The single top point where the triangular faces of a pyramid meet is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: The total area of just the side triangles, not the bottom.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El área total solo de los triángulos laterales, sin el fondo.
vocabulary.visual: For a square pyramid: lateral area = 4 × (½ × base edge × slant height)
vocabulary.cloze: The combined area of all the side faces, not counting the base, is the ___.

launch.narrative: Your class is building a pyramid-shaped display to showcase the time capsule at the school entrance. The pyramid will be covered in gold leaf to make it shine. To figure out how much gold leaf you need, you must calculate the total surface area — the base plus all the triangular faces!
launch.contextImage: Students assembling a square pyramid display covered in gold leaf for a time capsule exhibit
noticePrompts: How many faces does a square pyramid have?
What shape is the base? What shape are the side faces?
What is the difference between the height of the pyramid and the slant height?
wonderPrompts: Why do we use slant height instead of regular height for the triangular faces?
Would a taller pyramid always need more gold leaf than a shorter one?

explore.instructions: Calculate the surface area of each pyramid display. SA = Base Area + Lateral Area. For each triangular face, use A = ½ × base × slant height.

discourse.prompt: Display B has the largest surface area (280 in²). What contributes more to the total — the base or the lateral faces? Why?
discourse.sentenceFrame: For Display B, the base area is ___ in² and the total lateral area is 4 × ___ = ___ in². The ___ contribute(s) more because ___.

optional.stem: A square pyramid has a base that is 4 by 4 and 4 triangular faces each 10 sq units. What is the surface area?
optional.explanation: Base 4 × 4 = 16, plus 4 triangles × 10 = 40; total = 56 sq units.

optional.stem: How many triangular faces does a square pyramid have?
optional.explanation: A square pyramid has 4 triangular faces meeting at the top, plus 1 square base.

optional.stem: A triangular pyramid (all faces triangles) has 4 faces each with area 9 cm². What is the surface area?
To find a pyramid's surface area, you...
choices: Add the base area to the areas of all the triangular faces
Multiply length by width by height
Subtract the triangles from the base
optional.explanation: Surface area = base area + the areas of all the side (triangular) faces.

approaching.stem: A square pyramid has a base edge of 5 in and a slant height of 7 in. What is the surface area?
approaching.explanation: Base: 5 × 5 = 25 in². Each lateral face: ½ × 5 × 7 = 17.5 in². Four faces: 4 × 17.5 = 70 in². SA = 25 + 70 = 95 in².

approaching.stem: How many lateral (triangular) faces does a square pyramid have?
approaching.explanation: A square pyramid has a square base and 4 triangular lateral faces — one for each side of the square.

approaching.stem: What is the area of one triangular face with base 8 in and slant height 6 in?
approaching.explanation: Area of a triangle = ½ × base × height = ½ × 8 × 6 = 24 in².

approaching.instructions: Find the area of one lateral face for each pyramid.

approaching.label: Put the steps in order for finding the surface area of a square pyramid (base edge 6, slant height 10).

onLevel.instructions: Find the surface area of each square pyramid.

onLevel.stem: A square pyramid has a base area of 49 cm² and a total lateral area of 84 cm². What is the surface area?
onLevel.explanation: SA = Base Area + Lateral Area = 49 + 84 = 133 cm².

onLevel.label: A square pyramid has SA = 260 in². The base area is 100 in². What is the total lateral area? If there are 4 identical lateral faces, what is the area of one face?
onLevel.questionText: Total lateral area = ? in². One face = ? in².

onLevel.label: Sort these pyramids from LEAST surface area to GREATEST.

extending.title: Find the Surface Area Error

workedExample.label: Dimensions
workedExample.work: Square pyramid: base edge = 10 in, slant height = 8 in

workedExample.label: Lateral Area
workedExample.work: ½ × 10 × 8 = 40 in² (one face). Total lateral = 40 × 3 = 120 in²

extending.correctWork: A square pyramid has 4 lateral faces, not 3. Total lateral = 40 × 4 = 160 in². Correct: SA = 100 + 160 = 260 in².
hints: Check Step 3: how many lateral faces does a square pyramid have?
A square base has 4 sides, so there are 4 triangular faces. The student only multiplied by 3.

extending.prompt: Two pyramids have the same base area (64 in²) but different slant heights. Pyramid A has slant height 6 in and Pyramid B has slant height 10 in. How much more surface area does Pyramid B have? Why does slant height affect surface area but NOT base area?
extending.sentenceFrame: Pyramid A: SA = 64 + 4(½ × 8 × 6) = 64 + ___ = ___ in². Pyramid B: SA = 64 + 4(½ × 8 × 10) = 64 + ___ = ___ in². Pyramid B has ___ more in² because ___. Slant height only affects ___ because ___.

extending.instructions: Compare a square pyramid and a rectangular prism that sit on the same 6 × 6 base.

connect.scenario: A museum is building a square pyramid display case. The base edge is 3 feet and the slant height is 4 feet. The glass costs $12 per square foot.
connect.promptQuestion: How much will the glass for all 4 triangular sides cost? (The base is open — no glass needed.)
connect.prompt: Each triangular face has area = ½ × ___ × ___ = ___ ft². Four faces: 4 × ___ = ___ ft². Cost: ___ × $12 = $___.

exitTicket.stem: A square pyramid has a base edge of 6 in and a slant height of 5 in. What is the total surface area?
exitTicket.explanation: Base: 6 × 6 = 36 in². Each lateral face: ½ × 6 × 5 = 15 in². Four faces: 4 × 15 = 60 in². SA = 36 + 60 = 96 in². Surface area uses square units (in²).
